summary: The app writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Multimedia\Audio\DeviceCpl\ShowDisconnectedDevices = 1 to expose disconnected audio devices in the classic Sound control panel. Procmon captures on 2026-03-14 confirmed that rundll32.exe launching mmsys.cpl queries this exact value and reads both Data:1 and Data:0 when the value is toggled, so the registry surface is now validated as a live runtime preference on this build even though a primary Microsoft documentation page for the DeviceCpl contract was not captured.

setting.name: Show Disconnected Audio Devices
setting.casual_explanation: This makes disconnected audio devices appear in the classic Sound control panel. It is mainly useful for troubleshooting, advanced audio setup, or cleaning up stale devices.
setting.why_people_change_it: People enable it when they need to inspect endpoints that are currently unplugged or otherwise not active.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Showing disconnected devices can help debugging, but it can also expose stale or confusing entries that most users never need to see. This is a runtime-observed control-panel preference rather than a Microsoft-documented policy surface.
setting.professional_notes: The app writes ShowDisconnectedDevices = 1 under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Multimedia\Audio\DeviceCpl. On 2026-03-14, Procmon captures of rundll32.exe launching shell32.dll,Control_RunDLL mmsys.cpl,,0 showed RegQueryValue reads for ShowDisconnectedDevices with Data:1 and Data:0 when the value was toggled and restored. That proves the classic Sound control panel consumes this registry value on this build. No primary Microsoft Learn or ADMX source for the DeviceCpl contract was captured in this pass, so the record is validated as a runtime-observed preference rather than as a fully documented Microsoft contract.

allowed_values.label: Hide disconnected audio devices
allowed_values.meaning: Procmon confirmed that the classic Sound control panel reads ShowDisconnectedDevices = 0 on this build. This is the observed off state for the preference.

allowed_values.label: Show disconnected audio devices
allowed_values.meaning: Procmon confirmed that the classic Sound control panel reads ShowDisconnectedDevices = 1 on this build. This is the observed show-disconnected state used by the app.

targets.notes: Validated as a runtime-observed control-panel preference. The absent-value default was not captured in this pass.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes ShowDisconnectedDevices = 1, and Procmon confirmed that the classic Sound control panel reads the same value as a live preference on this build.

windows_defaults.label: Default state not captured
windows_defaults.applies_to: Classic Sound control panel profiles where the value is absent

states.rationale: This pass validated the 0 and 1 states through Procmon, but it did not capture the absent-value default.

windows_defaults.notes: Use this record as a validated runtime preference map for 0 and 1, not as a complete Microsoft-documented default model.

recommended_profiles.label: Show disconnected audio devices
avoid_for: General users who do not need extra endpoint visibility

states.rationale: Set the runtime-observed preference to the show-disconnected state that the Sound control panel reads on this build.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You need the classic Sound control panel to expose disconnected devices.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the cleanest possible device list with fewer stale entries.
tradeoffs: Disconnected devices become visible in the classic Sound control panel.
The endpoint list can become noisier or more confusing.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Sound control panel ShowDisconnectedDevices runtime reads
evidence.summary: Procmon captured rundll32.exe launching shell32.dll,Control_RunDLL mmsys.cpl,,0 and querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Multimedia\Audio\DeviceCpl\ShowDisconnectedDevices with Data:1 and Data:0 in separate reversible captures. That proves the classic Sound control panel consumes this registry value on this build.

decision.why: The registry path is validated as a live runtime preference because the classic Sound control panel queried ShowDisconnectedDevices with both 1 and 0 in reversible Procmon captures on this build, and the app writes the same show-disconnected state with a clean restore story.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: audio_devicecpl_query_20260314.pml: rundll32.exe RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Multimedia\Audio\DeviceCpl\ShowDisconnectedDevices Data:1. audio_devicecpl_query_zero_20260314.pml: rundll32.exe RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Multimedia\Audio\DeviceCpl\ShowDisconnectedDevices Data:0.
validation_proof.notes: The value was toggled from 1 to 0 and restored to 1 in reversible local captures. Both states were read by the classic Sound control panel on this build. Normalized for the consolidated evidence report.
